description: This schematic diagram illustrates the corrosion mechanism of Fe–30Mn–8Al–C low-density steels with 0 wt%, 1 wt%, and 3 wt% Cu (marked as 0Cu, 1Cu, 3Cu) exposed to 3.5 wt% NaCl solution. Left subpanels show early and late corrosion stages for each sample: 0Cu has sparse matrix carbides that initiate mild pitting at lattice distortion sites around carbides; 1Cu has Cu-refined dense dispersed carbides, which form multiple microgalvanic couples with the austenite matrix to generate more pitting sites; 3Cu contains undissolved elemental Cu enriched at grain boundaries alongside carbides, leading to synergistic microgalvanic corrosion that induces both intergranular corrosion and severe pitting. Right subpanels display the corresponding corroded surface morphologies for each sample. Four typical corrosion reaction equations for Fe, Al, Mn, and Cu during the corrosion process are also listed. This figure supports the core conclusion that increasing Cu addition deteriorates the corrosion resistance of Fe–Mn–Al–C low-density steel, with 3Cu exhibiting the worst corrosion performance.
